paper_title: Threshold Ramsey multiplicity for odd cycles
summary: Conjecture 4 from arXiv:2108.00987 asserts that m(C_k) = (k-1)!/2 for all sufficiently large odd k. The source paper establishes a lower bound m(C_k) >= (ck)^k (Theorem 3), while the upper bound (k-1)!/2 is achieved by the extremal coloring chi(k, k-1) of K_{2k-1}; the conjecture claims these match asymptotically. No follow-up paper proving or disproving the conjecture was found in a targeted web search across 2022–2026. The conjecture remains open.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch confirms this paper (Pure pairs. V. Excluding some long subdivision, Scott–Seymour–Spirkl) is entirely about pure pairs in perfect graphs and long-subdivision exclusions. It has no connection to Ramsey multiplicity for odd cycles, does not cite arXiv:2108.00987, and does not address Conjecture 4. The internal-reference match (fuzz=70) is a false positive.

search_queries: Ramsey multiplicity odd cycles conjecture Conlon Fox Sudakov Wei 2108.00987
threshold Ramsey multiplicity m(C_k) (k-1)!/2 odd cycle graph
Ramsey multiplicity odd cycles resolved proved 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture was found. The source paper was published in Revista de la Union Matematica Argentina (2022). The companion paper arXiv:2108.00991 addresses paths and even cycles but not this conjecture. The single internal reference (arXiv:2105.03956) is a fuzzy-match false positive unrelated to Ramsey multiplicity.
